faqs.answer: That question matters because New Jersey lenders often separate average-looking files from credible ones by whether the borrower has planned for tax reassessment risk, block-level comp review, and reserves tied to the exit path. The cleanest files connect scope, timeline, and exit math in one coherent narrative.
